name: searchoclock
description: On a Bash command failure, injects date-anchored deep-research troubleshooting context and dispatches a research subagent (searchoclock:searchoclock-researcher) to probe candidate fixes against live, dated sources and recommend the most durable one — then an INDEPENDENT second model (searchoclock:searchoclock-validator, Haiku 4.5) must agree from the raw evidence before the fix is trusted. Registers on PostToolUseFailure (primary) and PostToolUse (defensive), with an OPT-IN proactive PreToolUse pre-flight that nudges (non-blocking) on risky/version-sensitive commands before they run. Concurrency-safe for Opus 4.8 dynamic workflows / parallel agents; optional Q/T/R (quality/truthfulness/relevance) scorecard. Goal-scoped, inter-agent, fully standalone. Needs bash + python3; never blocks the agent loop.
